title: Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. X. Zu den ‘Signa ante Judicium’
summary: The repository XML is a local copy/version of University of Michigan CME item CME00100, a keyed encoding of Hermann Varnhagen’s complete 1880 Anglia article. Varnhagen prints one 398-line Fifteen Signs work from Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 and, continuously under a single Cotton heading, two distinct DIMEV works from British Library Cotton MS Caligula A II: a 56-line Invocation to the Creator and a 301-line Fifteen Signs work. The Cotton Fifteen Signs is compared with Edmund Stengel’s 1871 Digby 86 edition; Stengel, not Digby 86 directly, is Varnhagen’s cited comparative source. Complete public scans of Anglia volume 3, Stengel’s book, and Bodleian MS Digby 86 are cached outside Git at whole-object scope and mapped to the relevant pages or folios. No verified public whole-manuscript facsimile was found for Ff.2.38 or Cotton Caligula A II.
review_notes: The cataloging and lineage boundary is Varnhagen’s complete article, including its German preface, two edited witness sections, comparative note quotations, signature, and addendum; it is not one abstract medieval poem.

The first 74 direct Cotton lines form one XML LG but cross an abstract-work boundary: direct lines 1-56 are DIMEV 436 and direct lines 57-74 begin DIMEV 3000. The following 283-line LG continues DIMEV 3000.

The Cambridge foliation in Varnhagen and the 1857 catalog is historical. DIMEV’s current ff. 50va-53ra corresponds to historical ff. 42v-45r, a target-level offset of eight leaves after rebinding and refoliation.
Access and rights records apply only to the named entity or component. Public-domain medieval text does not make modern photographs, facsimiles, interfaces, encodings, or scholarship freely reusable.
Negative availability findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-13 and do not prove that no private, legacy, or unindexed reproduction exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00100
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository’s conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00100.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00100
entities.description: A publicly listed keyed and structurally marked-up digital text of Varnhagen’s complete article.
notes: The stable item is publicly listed, but its current interface returned an automated challenge during this audit; the pinned repository XML supplies exact content evidence.

entities.label: Varnhagen 1880 edition of two Middle English Fifteen Signs versions

bibliographic.citation: Hermann Varnhagen, ‘Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. X. Zu den “Signa ante Judicium”,’ Anglia 3 (1880): 533-551.
bibliographic.title: Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. X. Zu den ‘Signa ante Judicium’

notes: Printed page 533 contains the German preface; Cambridge text occupies pp. 534-542; the Cotton sequence occupies pp. 543-551; the closing signature and German addendum are on p. 551.
Varnhagen treats the two Cotton pieces continuously, while current DIMEV records them as distinct works 436 and 3000.

entities.label: BSB complete IIIF sequence for Anglia volume 3 (1880)
entities.description: A 618-canvas provider-published color sequence for the complete exposed journal object, not only Varnhagen’s article.
entities.reproduction_format: 618-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object; cached as a ZIP containing all width-1600 JPEG derivatives, provider manifest, and exact inventory
notes: Varnhagen’s article maps exactly to canvases 543-561, whose labels are printed pages 533-551.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: The sole DIMEV witness of the 398-line work beginning ‘The grace of the Holy Ghost.’ The target now occupies ff. 50va-53ra; Varnhagen and the 1857 catalog use the earlier foliation ff. 42v-45r.

holding.notes: The public item-level description remains primarily in the 1857 printed catalog; CUL states that most medieval manuscripts are not yet represented by modern ArchiveSearch records.

physical_description.extent: presently iii + 247 + i leaves, foliated 3-261 after rebinding and allowance for missing material
notes: The 1857 catalog described 247 leaves, double columns of about forty lines, a uniform mid-fifteenth-century hand, and missing leaves.
The Database of Middle English Romance reports two scribes and describes the codex as a broad religious, didactic, exemplary, and romance compilation.
DIMEV associates the target hand with LALME/eLALME LP 531, Grid 445 305, Leicestershire. This target-language localization is not a proven place of codex production.
Historical 42v corresponds to current 50v, historical 43 to current 51, historical 44 to current 52, and historical 45r to current 53r.
The first four printed lines are part of DIMEV 5309, not a separate invocation work.

entities.label: McSparran and Robinson 1979 paper facsimile of CUL MS Ff.2.38

bibliographic.citation: Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson, introduction, Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 (London: Scolar Press, 1979).
bibliographic.title: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38

entities.reproduction_format: 34 cm printed whole-manuscript facsimile with scholarly introduction
notes: ISBN 0859674711 / 9780859674713. No verified public complete digital copy was found.

entities.label: CUL manuscript catalog volume 2 (1857), Ff.2.38 entry

bibliographic.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vol. 2 (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1857), 404-408.
bibliographic.title: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge: Ee.I.1-Ff.vi.57

notes: Item 26 begins on historical f. 42b (42v) and is titled ‘The fifteen tokenys before the day of dome.’

entities.label: Dixon 2022 description of the current Ff.2.38 extent and foliation

bibliographic.citation: Katherine Ellen Dixon, ‘Leading by Example: The Pastoral Pedagogy of John Mirk’s Festial’ (PhD thesis, University of Cambridge, 2022), 157 n. 568.
bibliographic.title: Leading by Example: The Pastoral Pedagogy of John Mirk’s Festial

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A II
entities.description: The direct source of Varnhagen’s second edited sequence. DIMEV distinguishes a 56-line Invocation to the Creator at f. 89ra-rb (record 436) from a following 301-line Fifteen Signs work at ff. 89rb-91rb (record 3000), although the manuscript and Varnhagen present them continuously.

physical_description.extent: 210 folios in the present parent volume; the relevant Middle English component occupies ff. 3-139
notes: The parent catalog describes an intact volume in a 1957 British Museum binding, containing two principal items.
The component catalog dates ff. 3-139 to the second half of the fifteenth century and identifies England as origin.
DIMEV reports a small paraph before record 3000, Arabic numbers beside each of the fifteen days, and a large centered yellow-washed ‘Amen for charite’ at f. 91rb.
The current BL component record summarizes the Invocation at f. 89r and the Fifteen Signs at ff. 89r-91r; DIMEV gives the more exact ff. 89ra-rb and 89rb-91rb.
The XML/Varnhagen milestone 91v conflicts with the current catalog and DIMEV. The target is not extended into the Owayne Miles material that the BL catalog begins at f. 91v.

entities.label: Legacy selected reproduction of Cotton Caligula A II f. 93v
entities.description: A single public image reproduced through Wikimedia Commons from a plate in Robert Easting’s 1991 St Patrick’s Purgatory. It shows Owayne Miles material at f. 93v and has no CME00100 target overlap.
notes: This is not a current British Library digital surrogate and is not evidence that the whole manuscript has been digitized.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 86
entities.description: A collateral witness of DIMEV 3000 at ff. 120v-122v and the direct manuscript source of Stengel’s 1871 edition. Seventy lines are lost with the cut-away outer half of f. 121.

notes: Digital Bodleian assigns the manuscript to Worcestershire production in 1272-1282.
DIMEV/LAEME associates the target’s entry-1 language with Redmarley D’Abitot, north-west Gloucestershire, Grid 375 232; manuscript production and text-language localization remain separate claims.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Digby 86
entities.description: The complete 430-canvas Digital Bodleian object, including covers and every exposed digitized surface, cached at a practical width of 1600 pixels.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 430-canvas full-color IIIF sequence cached as width-1600 JPEG derivatives with manifest and inventory

entities.label: Stengel 1871 edition and description of Digby 86

bibliographic.citation: Edmund Stengel, Codicem manu scriptum Digby 86 in Bibliotheca Bodleiana asservatum descripsit, excerpsit, illustravit (Halle: Libraria Orphanotrophei, 1871).
bibliographic.title: Codicem manu scriptum Digby 86 in Bibliotheca Bodleiana asservatum descripsit, excerpsit, illustravit

notes: Varnhagen cites Stengel’s pp. 53 ff. as the printed Digby fragment related to his Cotton text; he does not claim a new direct collation of Digby 86.

entities.label: BSB complete IIIF sequence for Stengel’s Digby 86 book (1871)
entities.description: A 154-canvas provider-published color sequence for the complete exposed printed book.
entities.reproduction_format: 154-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object; cached as width-1600 JPEG derivatives with manifest and inventory
notes: Stengel’s target text on printed pp. 53-57 maps exactly to canvases 71-75.

entities.label: DIMEV records 5309, 436, and 3000
entities.description: The version 1.0 Digital Index of Middle English Verse records the Cambridge 398-line work as 5309 / IMEV-NIMEV 3368; the Cotton invocation as 436 / IMEV-NIMEV 256; and the following Cotton and Digby Fifteen Signs tradition as 3000 / IMEV-NIMEV 1823.
notes: Record 5309 has one witness, CUL Ff.2.38, current ff. 50va-53ra, 398 couplet lines, edited by Varnhagen pp. 534-542.
Record 436 has three witnesses; its Cotton version is 28 couplets at f. 89ra-rb and is treated in that manuscript as an introduction to record 3000.
Record 3000 has six active witnesses in version 1.0. Its Cotton version occupies ff. 89rb-91rb; its Digby version occupies ff. 120v-122v and loses seventy lines with the outer half of f. 121.

access.access_method: Submit a bespoke imaging request through CUL’s Cultural Heritage Imaging Laboratory portal. Request the complete presently bound object separately from the target current ff. 50va-53ra / historical ff. 42v-45r and request written publication and redistribution terms.

Complex handling is listed at £58.19. BL’s January 2026 terms state a standard thirty-working-day service after payment and require written permission for copying or reproduction.

rights.component: The three underlying anonymous medieval works and their medieval witness readings
rights.basis: The texts and surviving manuscript witnesses are medieval and predate modern copyright by centuries.
notes: This conclusion does not govern modern photographs, facsimiles, scholarly editions, encodings, interfaces, or provider terms.

rights.component: Varnhagen’s 1880 editorial text, introduction, notes, arrangement, and addendum
rights.basis: The article was published in 1880, far before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: Stengel’s 1871 editorial, descriptive, and transcribed text
rights.basis: The book was published in 1871, far before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: University of Michigan CME digital text and collection presentation
rights.basis: The current CME collection describes its manually keyed source editions as public domain and applies No Copyright - United States; exact item-level confirmation was impeded by the automated interface challenge.

rights.component: Pinned XML copy and its U-M-derived encoded text
rights.basis: The artifact is an exact repository representation of the U-M CME encoding whose collection is marked No Copyright - United States; no separate content restriction is stated in the XML.
notes: The repository’s GPL-3.0 statement governs its code and does not by itself resolve every source-data layer.

rights.component: BSB Anglia volume 3 digital images and IIIF reproduction layer
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest applies the No Copyright-Non-Commercial Use Only rights statement; it is not an open commercial-use license.
notes: Do not include the cached images in commercial print-on-demand output without checking current BSB terms and obtaining any needed permission.

rights.component: BSB Stengel-book digital images and IIIF reproduction layer
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest applies the No Copyright-Non-Commercial Use Only rights statement; it is not an open commercial-use license.

rights.component: Complete Digital Bodleian manuscript image sequence
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest attribution applies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International to the photographs.
notes: Attribution and noncommercial conditions apply; the photographs are not authorized for for-profit print-on-demand reuse without separate permission.

rights.basis: The versioned release includes a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 license.

rights.component: Any images supplied through Cambridge University Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: CUL distinguishes private-study or noncommercial research supply from publication and commercial licensing; no item-specific license has been issued.
notes: Obtain written terms before redistributing supplied images in the repository or using them in any commercial publication workflow.

rights.component: 1979 introduction, design, and printed facsimile reproduction
rights.jurisdiction: Applicable law and publisher or rights-holder terms
rights.basis: The facsimile was published in 1979 and no open license or public-domain assertion was found in the consulted records.
notes: Owning or consulting the printed facsimile does not grant a right to scan and redistribute it.

rights.component: Any future British Library photographs or supplied digital facsimile
rights.basis: No public whole-manuscript image object or item-specific license was available. BL’s imaging terms prohibit copying or reproduction without written permission and separate publication guidance applies.
notes: Request explicit repository redistribution and commercial print-on-demand terms; digitisation supply alone is not publication permission.

rights.component: Legacy selected f. 93v reproduction hosted by Wikimedia Commons
rights.basis: The Commons file page applies the Public Domain Mark to the image.
notes: The image derives from a plate in a 1991 Oxford University Press book, is not a current BL surrogate, and has no CME00100 target overlap.

rights.basis: The catalog volume was published in 1857, far before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: Dixon’s 2022 thesis text and repository PDF
rights.basis: Public repository access does not itself place the modern thesis in the public domain or grant unrestricted republication.

editorial_practices.description: Varnhagen selects two Middle English manuscript treatments of the Fifteen Signs and presents the Cotton invocation continuously with its following signs text; he does not reproduce either complete codex.
Varnhagen compares the Cotton text with Stengel’s printed Digby fragment and cites other analogues, but leaves the priority between the Cotton and Digby versions unresolved.
notes: ‘Collation’ here describes printed comparative work, not a claim that Varnhagen directly inspected Digby 86.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned source, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00100.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00100, Varnhagen’s title and publication, German and Middle English language declaration, the complete printed-page sequence 533-551, the Cambridge and Cotton headings, historical folio milestones, signature, and addendum.

evidence.citation: Structural and boundary audit of the pinned CME00100 XML.
evidence.locator: P, LG, L, NOTE/LG/L, PB, MILESTONE, and direct Cotton L boundaries
evidence.summary: The XML has three P elements, six LG elements, 763 total L elements, 755 direct/base verse lines, and eight verse lines nested in two German notes. Cambridge has two LGs of 4 and 394 lines. Cotton has direct LGs of 74 and 283 lines; direct Cotton lines 1-56 are DIMEV 436 and lines 57-74 plus the 283-line LG are DIMEV 3000.
notes: Cotton direct line 56 ends ‘Thorow Adam synne and Eve also’; direct line 57 begins ‘Kyng of heven, blessed þou be.’

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Middle English Compendium, CME source-file list with URLs, entry CME00100.
evidence.summary: Lists CME00100 and supplies its stable U-M item link and edition-level identity.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection description and rights statement.
evidence.locator: Collection description, access statement, and rights and permissions
evidence.summary: Describes manually keyed full texts from public-domain editions, public web access and bulk XML availability, and applies No Copyright - United States to the collection presentation.

evidence.citation: Hermann Varnhagen, ‘Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. X. Zu den “Signa ante Judicium”,’ Anglia 3 (1880): 533-551.
evidence.summary: Immediate printed source for the complete CME item, the Cambridge and Cotton source claims, Varnhagen’s comparison with Stengel’s Digby text, historical foliation, editorial notes, signature, and addendum.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF manifest for bsb11642505, Anglia volume 3 (1880).
evidence.summary: Supplies complete carrier identity, canvas labels and image services, provider attribution, NoC-NC rights statement, and the exact whole-object cache inventory.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the first and last pages of Varnhagen’s article in the BSB sequence.
evidence.summary: Canvas 543 is printed p. 533 and begins the titled article and German preface; canvas 561 is printed p. 551 and contains the end of the Cotton text, Varnhagen’s signature, and the addendum.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 5309, 436, and 3000; manuscript entries CULFf238, BlCottCalAII, BodDig86, and linked collateral witnesses; bibliography entries Varnhagen1880, Stengel1871, and McSparran1979a.
evidence.summary: Supplies the three abstract-work boundaries, IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, incipits, descriptions, couplet forms, line counts, witness censuses and exact loci, manuscript-title details, linguistic-profile distinctions, edition and facsimile references, and CC BY 4.0 release license.

evidence.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vol. 2 (Cambridge, 1857), Ff.2.38 entry, pp. 404-408.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 404-408; PDF pp. 416-420; item 26 on printed p. 407
evidence.summary: Describes Ff.2.38 as a 247-leaf mid-fifteenth-century paper folio in double columns, identifies missing leaves, surveys its contents, and places ‘The fifteen tokenys before the day of dome’ at historical f. 42b.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, ‘Search the Collections’ for archives and medieval manuscripts.
evidence.locator: medieval-manuscript search guidance and linked old catalog volumes
evidence.summary: States that most medieval manuscripts remain cataloged only in the five nineteenth-century printed volumes and that not all holdings appear in ArchiveSearch; links the public scans.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Archives, Modern and Medieval Manuscripts and University Archives department page.
evidence.locator: Special Collections Reading Room access and advance ordering
evidence.summary: Provides the physical consultation route and directs readers to order manuscripts in advance through specialcollections@lib.cam.ac.uk.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library Cultural Heritage Imaging Laboratory, ordering images and online request portal.
evidence.locator: bespoke imaging, formats, price minimum, quotation and delivery, private-study and publication-use options

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A II, ff. 3-139 component record.
evidence.locator: ff. 3-139 component; Invocation f. 89r; Fifteen Signs ff. 89r-91r; adjacent items
evidence.summary: Dates the component 1450-1499 in England and identifies the current broad target loci. It also places a song to the Virgin at f. 91r and Owayne Miles from f. 91v, constraining interpretation of the XML’s conflicting 91v milestone.

evidence.citation: British Library, digitised manuscripts list, updated 10 October 2025.
evidence.locator: complete 261-page alphabetical list; Cotton Caligula A II absent
evidence.summary: The current public list contains no Cotton Caligula A II entry; together with the exact catalog’s blank Digitised Content field, this supports the date-bounded negative availability finding.

evidence.summary: Provides the current request route for consulting Western Manuscripts in London, subject to Reader Pass and supporting-document requirements.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services.
evidence.locator: service description, online order form, manuscript-original handling, color request, and publication contact
evidence.summary: Provides the official reproduction-order route and explains that original manuscripts require photographic assessment rather than assumed scanning; publication permission is a separate enquiry.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services price list, 2025/26.
evidence.summary: Lists general JPEG scan, photographic TIFF, and complex-handling charges. The rates are not a Cotton Caligula A II quote and scan pricing cannot be assumed applicable to a manuscript original.

evidence.citation: British Library Imaging Services terms, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: States a standard thirty-working-day service following payment and prohibits copying or reproduction of supplied material without written permission; the page names permissions@bl.uk while the service page currently directs publication enquiries to licensing@bl.uk.

evidence.citation: Edmund Stengel, Codicem manu scriptum Digby 86 in Bibliotheca Bodleiana asservatum descripsit, excerpsit, illustravit (Halle, 1871), 53-57.
evidence.summary: Prints the Digby 86 Fifteen Signs fragment cited by Varnhagen and establishes Stengel’s role as its editor and transcriber.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF manifest for bsb11013506, Stengel’s Digby 86 book (1871).
evidence.summary: Supplies complete printed-book identity, canvas labels and image services, provider attribution, NoC-NC rights statement, and the exact whole-object cache inventory.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of Stengel’s target text in the BSB sequence.
evidence.summary: Printed p. 53 begins ‘Les XV singnes de domesday’; printed p. 57 ends the target text before the following item.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright (revised April 2026).
evidence.locator: published works and current public-domain cutoff
evidence.summary: States that all works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain. The much earlier publication dates of the 1857, 1871, and 1880 sources make their U.S. public-domain status secure; this does not decide modern-image or provider-contract rights.

open_questions.question: When were DIMEV 5309, 436, and 3000 composed, and what is the historical relationship among their Fifteen Signs versions?
next_steps: Consult modern Fifteen Signs scholarship and all DIMEV witness editions.
Keep witness dates separate from composition dates and avoid deriving one abstract poem from Varnhagen’s article boundary.
notes: Varnhagen explicitly leaves priority between the Cotton and Digby-related forms undecided.

open_questions.question: Why does the XML/Varnhagen sequence mark 91v near the Cotton ending when the current BL catalog ends the Fifteen Signs on 91r and DIMEV gives 91rb?
next_steps: Inspect a current color facsimile of ff. 89r-91v.
Compare Varnhagen’s foliation and line placement against the present BL binding and cataloging.
notes: Until resolved, use DIMEV’s precise ff. 89rb-91rb for the target and do not absorb Owayne Miles at f. 91v.

open_questions.question: Does CUL hold unpublished whole-codex photography or a digitized copy of the 1979 Ff.2.38 facsimile, and what reuse terms would apply?
next_steps: Ask CUL Special Collections and Imaging Services about complete existing photography, microfilm, or an internal surrogate.
Ask the relevant rights holder whether the 1979 facsimile may be digitized or licensed for research access.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply a complete color facsimile of Cotton Caligula A II, and would its written terms permit repository redistribution or commercial print-on-demand use?
next_steps: Request a shelfmark-specific quote for the complete object and separately identify target ff. 89ra-91rb.
Request explicit written terms for Git repository access, research use, self-publishing, and for-profit print-on-demand use before distributing any supplied files.

open_questions.question: What is the most reliable dialect or text-language localization for the Cotton copies of DIMEV 436 and 3000?
next_steps: Consult LALME/eLALME, LAEME where applicable, and manuscript-specific linguistic scholarship.
Do not transfer Digby’s north-west Gloucestershire target-language localization or Worcestershire production to the Cotton text.

open_questions.question: Which exact editions or witnesses supplied the eight Middle English lines quoted in Varnhagen’s Cursor Mundi and Hending notes?
next_steps: Resolve ‘Curs. Mundi 22493 ff.’ and ‘Lond. text 176’ against Varnhagen’s bibliography and the other sections of Anglia volume 3.
Add supporting edition entities only after the exact quoted source layers are confirmed.
